content:
The user wants me to extract the table from the provided image.

1.  **Identify the table:** The image contains a table titled "Table 1. Pin function list".
2.  **Determine columns:** The columns are QFN, CSP, Name, Type, Description, and Typical connection.
3.  **Extract rows:** I need to go through each row and extract the text content.

*   **Header:** QFN, CSP, Name, Type, Description, Typical connection
*   **Row 1:** 1, B4, CC1DB, HV AIO, Dead battery enable on CC1 pin, To CC1 pin if used or ground
*   **Row 2:** 2, B5, CC1, HV AIO, Type-C configuration channel 1, To Type-C receptacle A5
*   **Row 3:** 3, B3, C3, NC, -, Floating
*   **Row 4:** 4, C5, CC2, HV AIO, Type-C configuration channel 2, To Type-C receptacle B5
*   **Row 5:** 5, C4, CC2DB, HV AIO, Dead battery enable on CC2 pin, To CC2 pin if used or ground
*   **Row 6:** 6, D4, RESET, DI, Reset input, active high, From system
*   **Row 7:** 7, D5, SCL, DI, I²C clock input, To I²C master, ext. pull-up
*   **Row 8:** 8, E5, SDA, DI/OD, I²C data input/output, active low open drain, To I²C master, ext. pull-up
*   **Row 9:** 9, E4, DISCH, HV AI/OD, Internal discharge path or external discharge path enable, active low open drain, From power system (internal path) or to the discharge path switch (external path), ext. pull-up
*   **Row 10:** 10, E3, GND, GND, Ground, Ground
*   **Row 11:** 11, E2, ATTACH, OD, Attachment detection, active low open drain, To MCU if any, ext. pull-up
*   **Row 12:** 12, D3, ADDR0, DI, I²C device address setting, Static, to ground or ext. pull-up for address selection, to ground if no connection to MCU
*   **Row 13:** 13, D2, ADDR1, DI, I²C device address setting, Static, to ground or ext. pull-up for address selection, to ground if no connection to MCU
*   **Row 14:** 14, E1, POWER_OK3, OD, Power contract flag, active low open drain, To power system, ext. pull-up
*   **Row 15:** 15, D1, GPIO, OD, General purpose output, active low open drain, To system, ext. pull-up
*   **Row 16:** 16, C1, VBUS_EN_SNK, HV OD, VBUS sink power path enable, active low open drain, To power switch or to power system, ext. pull-up
*   **Row 17:** 17, C2, A_B_SIDE, OD, Cable orientation, active low open drain, USB super speed MUX select, ext. pull-up
*   **Row 18:** 18, A1, VBUS_VS_DISCH, HV AI, VBUS voltage monitoring and discharge path, From VBUS, receptacle side
*   **Row 19:** 19, B2, ALERT, OD, I²C interrupt, active low open drain, To I²C master, ext. pull-up
*   **Row 20:** 20, A2, POWER_OK2, HV OD, Power contract flag, active low open drain, To power switch or to power system, ext. pull-up
*   **Row 21:** 21, A3, VREG_1V2, PWR, 1.2 V internal regulator output, 1 µF typ. decoupling capacitor
*   **Row 22:** 22, B1, VSYS, PWR, Power supply from system, From power system, connect to ground if not used
*   **Row 23:** 23, A4, VREG_2V7, PWR, 2.7 V internal regulator output, 1 µF typ. decoupling capacitor
*   **Row 24:** 24, A5, VDD, HV PWR, Power supply from USB power line, From VBUS, receptacle side
*   **Row 25:** EP, -, EP, GND, Exposed pad is connected to ground, To ground

4.  **Format as Markdown:** Convert the extracted data into a Markdown table.
5.  **Check for footers/headers:** Add the document title and any other relevant text. The document title is "STUSB4500 Pinout". The footer contains "DS12499 - Rev 8" and "page 4/42".

Let's assemble the markdown.
```markdown
| QFN | CSP | Name | Type | Description | Typical connection |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | B4 | CC1DB | HV AIO | Dead battery enable on CC1 pin | To CC1 pin if used or ground |
| 2 | B5 | CC1 | HV AIO | Type-C configuration channel 1 | To Type-C receptacle A5 |
| 3 | B3, C3 | NC | - | - | Floating |
| 4 | C5 | CC2 | HV AIO | Type-C configuration channel 2 | To Type-C receptacle B5 |
| 5 | C4 | CC2DB | HV AIO | Dead battery enable on CC2 pin | To CC2 pin if used or ground |
| 6 | D4 | RESET | DI | Reset input, active high | From system |
| 7 | D5 | SCL | DI | I²C clock input | To I²C master, ext. pull-up |
| 8 | E5 | SDA | DI/OD | I²C data input/output, active low open drain | To I²C master, ext. pull-up |
| 9 | E4 | DISCH | HV AI/OD | Internal discharge path or external discharge path enable, active low open drain | From power system (internal path) or to the discharge path switch (external path), ext. pull-up |
| 10 | E3 | GND | GND | Ground | Ground |
| 11 | E2 | ATTACH | OD | Attachment detection, active low open drain | To MCU if any, ext. pull-up |
| 12 | D3 | ADDR0 | DI | I²C device address setting | Static, to ground or ext. pull-up for address selection, to ground if no connection to MCU |
| 13 | D2 | ADDR1 | DI | I²C device address setting | Static, to ground or ext. pull-up for address selection, to ground if no connection to MCU |
| 14 | E1 | POWER_OK3 | OD | Power contract flag, active low open drain | To power system, ext. pull-up |
| 15 | D1 | GPIO | OD | General purpose output, active low open drain | To system, ext. pull-up |
| 16 | C1 | VBUS_EN_SNK | HV OD | VBUS sink power path enable, active low open drain | To power switch or to power system, ext. pull-up |
| 17 | C2 | A_B_SIDE | OD | Cable orientation, active low open drain | USB super speed MUX select, ext. pull-up |
| 18 | A1 | VBUS_VS_DISCH | HV AI | VBUS voltage monitoring and discharge path | From VBUS, receptacle side |
| 19 | B2 | ALERT | OD | I²C interrupt, active low open drain | To I²C master, ext. pull-up |
| 20 | A2 | POWER_OK2 | HV OD | Power contract flag, active low open drain | To power switch or to power system, ext. pull-up |
| 21 | A3 | VREG_1V2 | PWR | 1.2 V internal regulator output | 1 µF typ. decoupling capacitor |
| 22 | B1 | VSYS | PWR | Power supply from system | From power system, connect to ground if not used |
| 23 | A4 | VREG_2V7 | PWR | 2.7 V internal regulator output | 1 µF typ. decoupling capacitor |
| 24 | A5 | VDD | HV PWR | Power supply from USB power line | From VBUS, receptacle side |
| EP | - | EP | GND | Exposed pad is connected to ground | To ground |
```
Double check the content. Looks accurate.
The title "Table 1. Pin function list" is above the table.
The logo "ST" is in the top left.
The product name "STUSB4500 Pinout" is in the top right.
The footer "DS12499 - Rev 8" is in the bottom left.
The page number "page 4/42" is in the bottom right.

I will output the markdown table as requested.
One small detail: "B3, C3" in row 3.
One small detail: "HV AI/OD" in row 9.
One small detail: "I²C" uses the special character.
One small detail: "µF" uses the special character.
One small detail: "V_{BUS}" is written as VBUS in the table.

Final check of the table structure.
Columns: QFN, CSP, Name, Type, Description, Typical connection.
Rows: 25 data rows + 1 header row.
Looks good.
I will now generate the response.
```
